D9 Biology L5 Evaluate Reference

Quorum Sensing — Vibrio Counts Itself

Vibrio fischeri bacteria release autoinducer molecules; only when density crosses a threshold does enough autoinducer bind back, switching luminescence genes on for the whole colony

Each Vibrio fischeri continuously secretes a small molecule (autoinducer-1, a homoserine lactone). At low density it diffuses away; at high density it accumulates, binds back to the LuxR receptor, activates the lux operon (luciferase + accessory enzymes) — all cells suddenly light up together. Used by the bobtail squid Euprymna for counterillumination camouflage.

Drag bacterial density. Below threshold (~10⁶ cells/mL) no light; above it the entire population switches on sharply.
